name: Waterfall Snoqualmie
location: Snoqualmie, Washington (30 miles east of Seattle)

geology.origin: Fissure eruptions, eastern Washington/Oregon, flowed west
geology.mechanism: Hard basalt cliff over softer valley; river runs off vertical edge

tribal_significance.significance: Origin place -- where Transformer created first human beings in Snoqualmie cosmology
tribal_significance.practice: Living spiritual practice: prayer, purification, ceremony. Not historical -- active today.
tribal_significance.treaty: Point Elliott Treaty (1855): ceded territory; retained cultural access
tribal_significance.contemporary: Tribe headquartered in Snoqualmie, WA. Language revitalization and land stewardship active.

pop_culture.role: Opening image of every episode -- visual signature of the show
pop_culture.filming_location: Salish Lodge (above the falls) = Great Northern Hotel in show
pop_culture.why_chosen: Falls plunges into unseen gorge -- visual metaphor for Twin Peaks' central theme: power beyond sight, in unreachable places
pop_culture.influence: Pioneered serialized TV drama; direct ancestor of The Sopranos, Lost, Breaking Bad

hydroelectric.type: Underground powerhouse (generators carved into basalt cliff)
hydroelectric.note: First underground powerhouse type in world; water diversion reduces visible flow

acoustic_profile.type: White noise -- equal energy at all audible frequencies

theory_nodes.concept: Sacred + physical: Snoqualmie Falls is simultaneously the Snoqualmie Tribe's origin point (Sahalie Tyee, the Transformer) and a 268-foot geological feature and a Puget Sound Energy power plant and a Twin Peaks filming location. The same physical place holds all these meanings simultaneously.
theory_nodes.description: Layered meanings at a single geographic point.

theory_nodes.concept: White noise acoustics: a large waterfall produces equal energy at all audible frequencies simultaneously. No dominant pitch. The acoustic antithesis of a biological vocalization (which is specific-frequency, structured, communicative). The falls' sound is the most complete abiotic soundscape the engine will document.

Twin Peaks and the falls: Lynch chose Snoqualmie Falls because it plunges into an unseen gorge. The visual inaccessibility -- power beyond sight -- is the show's central metaphor. The most culturally famous PNW waterfall is famous for a quality the SPS engine also values: what you cannot fully perceive.
theory_nodes.description: Why Twin Peaks chose Snoqualmie Falls.

cross_references.relationship: The same Snoqualmie River system, upstream. Distributed polyrhythm (245) vs. concentrated white-noise wall (246).
The waterfall arc continues. Snoqualmie (268 ft, basalt, cultural saturation) vs. Sol Duc (Olympic canyon, resonant, rainforest).

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. Perfect equilibrium at high energy. Raw power in formation: the falls is always the falls; Hendrix became Hendrix in London. Both are overwhelmingly powerful forces awaiting their full architectural frame.
